# @andes/badge

Badge Component

Badges are small units of information that allow the status of an item to be indicated or to differentiate several similar items from one another.
The UI specs are defined in [Andes UI](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/960764-badge/b/26f535).

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
$ npm install --save @andes/badge
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/badge/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```scss
// Base
@import '@andes/badge/base';

// By modifier
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/modifier/dot';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/modifier/pill';

// By size
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/size/small';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/size/large';

// By color
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/informative';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/neutral';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/positive';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/caution';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/negative';
@import '@andes/badge/lib/styles/color/inverted';
```

> Always import @andes/badge/base and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Dot, Pill } from '@andes/badge';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Badge](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/badge-dot--andes-badge-dot).

### Pill

```jsx
import { Pill } from '@andes/badge';

<Pill
  color='positive'
  size='small'
  hierarchy='quiet'
>
  NUEVO
</Pill>
```

### PillIcon

```jsx
import { PillIcon } from '@andes/badge';

<PillIcon
  color='positive'
/>

// improve screen reader user's experience
<PillIcon color='negative' srLabel={i18n.gettext('Error')} />
```

### Dot

```jsx
import { Dot } from '@andes/badge';

<Dot color='positive'/>
```

## Props

### Pill

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content (only available for `contentType=text`) | `node` | `undefined` | |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `color` | <sup>1</sup>Color of the Pill, to show different states | `string: 'caution', 'informative', 'inverted', 'negative', 'neutral', 'positive'` | `undefined` | |
| `contentType` | <sup>2</sup>Pill content type | `string: 'icon', 'text'` | `'text'` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Style of the Pill (only available for `contentType=text`) | `string: 'loud', 'quiet'` | `'loud'` | |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Size of the Pill | `string: 'large', 'small'` | `'large'` |  |
| `roundedCorners` | Corners of the Pill that should be rounded | `arrayOf: 'top-left', 'top-right', 'bottom-left', 'bottom-right'` | `['top-left', 'top-right', 'bottom-left', 'bottom-right']` |  |
| `secondaryColor` | <sup>3</sup>Allows to use a custom color from secondary palette (only available for `contentType=text`) | `object<ColorConfig>` | `undefined` | |
| `srLabel` | <sup>4</sup>Label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` | |

#### ColorConfig

```jsx
  {
    background: string, // Background color of the pill
    text: string, // Text color of the pill
  }
```

> 1 - `color` value `neutral` is not available for `contentType=icon`

> 2 - `contentType` will be deprecated in a future major version, please use `PillIcon` instead of `contentType='icon'` or remove it if you're using `contentType='text'`.

> 3 - `secondaryColor` prop should only use colors from the secondary palette:
- For the “Loud” hierarchy use the base value (500) or higher values ​​for padding.
- For the “Quiet” hierarchy, use the value 100 or the lighter values ​​up to 300 for the fill and for the text color, use the value 600 onwards.
  Try to keep a distance of 4 values ​​between the fill and text color.
- The combinations must be between values ​​of the same color. Mixtures between colors are not allowed.

> 4 - You can use 'srLabel' prop in order to improve screen reader user's experience, for example, describing the current badge status. E.g.:

```jsx
  <Pill
    color='negative'
    srLabel={i18n.gettext('Error: No hay mas stock')}
  >
    No hay más stock
  </Pill>
```

### Dot

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `color` | Color of the dot, to show different states | `string: 'caution', 'informative', 'inverted', 'negative', 'neutral', 'positive'` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `srLabel` | <sup>1</sup>Label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` | |

> 1 - You can use `srLabel` prop in order to improve screen reader user's experience, for example, describing the current badge status. E.g.:

```jsx
  <Dot
    color='positive'
    srLabel={i18n.gettext('Estado: correcto')}
  />
```

### PillIcon

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `color` | Color of the PillIcon, to show different states | `string: 'caution', 'informative', 'negative', 'positive'` | `undefined` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `size` | Size of the PillIcon | `string: 'large', 'small', 'xsmall'` | `'large'` |  |
| `srLabel` | <sup>1</sup>Label for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` | |

> 1- You can use 'srLabel' prop in order to improve screen reader user's experience, for example, describing the current badge status. E.g.:

```jsx
  <PillIcon
    color='negative'
    srLabel={i18n.gettext('Error: No hay mas stock')}
  >
    No hay más stock
  </PillIcon>
```

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

Badge allows you to modify the following attributes.

| Sub Attribute | Description       |
| ------------- | ----------------- |
| `fill`        | Badge fill color. |
| `text`        | Badge text color. |
| `icon`        | Badge icon color. |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      [referenceValue]: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            [feedbackValue]: {
              [context]: colorValue,
            },
          },
          text: {
            [feedbackValue]: {
              [context]: colorValue,
            },
          },
          icon: {
            [feedbackValue]: {
              [context]: colorValue,
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

The possible `referenceValue` values ​​are:

```jsx

referenceValue: loud | quiet | dot;

```

The possible `feedbackValue` values ​​are:

```jsx

status: informative | positive | caution | negative | neutral | inverted;

```

The possible `context` values ​​are:

```jsx

context: base | onComplexBg;

```

> More states may be added in the future, so we recommend consulting the @andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json file for more detailed information

### Example

<img src="resources/badge/docs/Badge-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Badge Example" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Pill } from '@andes/badge';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      loud: {
        color: {
          text: {
            caution: {
              base: '#FFC107',
            },
          },
          fill: {
            caution: {
              base: '#FFEB3B',
            },
          },
          icon: {
            caution: {
              base: '#FFC107',
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Pill color="caution">NUEVO</Pill>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/badge/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes badge regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/badge/docs/Badge-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming Badge Example" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Pill } from '@andes/badge';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      backgroundColor: '#8cb4f0',
      borderRadius: '0',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Pill>NUEVO</Pill>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/badge/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute               | Description                                                                                    |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-badge`           | Badge tag.                                                                                     |
| `data-andes-badge-type`      | Badge type. Can be: `'pill'`, `'icon'` or `'dot'`.                                             |
| `data-andes-badge-hierarchy` | Badge hierarchy. Can be: `'loud'` or `'quiet'`. It doesn't apply to all type of badges         |
| `data-andes-badge-size`      | Badge size. Can be: `'xsmall'`, `'small'` or `'large'`. It doesn't apply to all type of badges |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

### Example

<img src="resources/badge/docs/Badge-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Badge Example" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Dot } from '@andes/badge';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-badge-type="dot"]': {
          width: '16px',
          height: '16px',
          border: '2px solid red',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Dot/>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/badge/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
